content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                Date : June 29, 1979


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Lee C. Rosenthal

Subject: Lobbyist Employers

     Seven title insurance companies wish to lobby a bill.
They hire a law firm which, in turn, hires a lobbying firm
to represent the interests of the title companies. The
actual lobbyist, of course, is an employee of the lobbying
firm.

     Under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619, I believe that
both the law firm and the lobbying firm are retained entities
and that the title insurance firms are employers of the lobbyist. If this is not the case, then so long as the title
insurance firms paid less than $250 a month there would be
nothing to associate the title firms with the lobbying
effort and the law would be effectively evaded.

LCR:plh


7/2/79

Staff Advice:

The law firm and the seven title insurance companies are
employers of a lobbyist under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18619(b). This decision is based upon the staff's opinion
that the title insurance companies intended to employ a
lobbyist and retained the law firm only for that purpose.

sections.question: Whether the law firm and lobbying firm retained by seven title insurance companies to lobby a bill constitute retained entities, and whether the title insurance firms are employers of a lobbyist under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619.
sections.conclusion: The law firm and the seven title insurance companies are employers of a lobbyist under 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18619(b). This decision is based upon the staff's opinion that the title insurance companies intended to employ a lobbyist and retained the law firm only for that purpose.
sections.question_synthetic: Are seven title insurance companies that hired a law firm, which in turn hired a lobbying firm to represent their interests in a bill, considered employers of a lobbyist under California Administrative Code Section 18619?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the title insurance companies are employers of a lobbyist under Section 18619(b), as they intentionally retained the law firm specifically to employ a lobbyist to represent their lobbying interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format with informal presentation of question followed by staff advice section. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers, but structure is clear. Document shows FPPC analysis of lobbying employer definitions.

CONCLUSION: The law firm and the seven title insurance companies are employers of a lobbyist under 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18619(b). This decision is based upon the staff's opinion that the title insurance companies intended to employ a lobbyist and retained the law firm only for that purpose.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that seven title insurance companies that hired a law firm to hire a lobbying firm are employers of a lobbyist under California Administrative Code Section 18619(b), based on the companies' intent to employ a lobbyist through the law firm arrangement.
